phrases.text_local: Quand a lieu le marché aux puces ?
phrases.pronunciation: kohn ah lee-OO luh mar-SHAY oh poos
phrases.cultural_note: The Marolles flea market is held every Sunday from 6am to 2pm. It's one of Europe's largest and most famous flea markets.

responses.english: Every day, 6am to 2pm. Sunday is the best day.
responses.phonetic: too lay zhoor, duh see zuhr ah kah-torz uhr. luh dee-MAHNSH eh luh may-YUHR zhoor
responses.french: Tous les jours, de six heures à quatorze heures. Le dimanche est le meilleur jour.

phrases.text_local: Où est le point de vue célèbre ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo eh luh pwan duh VOO say-LEBR
phrases.cultural_note: The 'Place du Jeu de Balle' offers one of Brussels' most iconic views: a flea market scene with the Palace of Justice in the background.

responses.english: From Place du Jeu de Balle, you have the Palace of Justice in the background.
responses.phonetic: duh-PWEE lah PLAHS doo zhuh duh BAL, voo zah-VAY luh pah-LAY duh zhoos-TEES ahn ah-ryehr-PLAHN
responses.french: Depuis la Place du Jeu de Balle, vous avez le Palais de Justice en arrière-plan.

phrases.cultural_note: Bargaining is expected and accepted. Start at about 60% of the asking price. Be friendly — vendors appreciate good humor.

responses.english: Yes, you can negotiate. Offer a bit less.
responses.phonetic: wee, ohn puh nay-goh-SYAY. proh-poh-ZAY uhn puh MWAHN
responses.french: Oui, on peut négocier. Proposez un peu moins.

phrases.cultural_note: The market is great for vinyl records, vintage clothing, old posters, and antique furniture. Quality varies — dig deep!

responses.english: Records, clothes, posters, and antique furniture.
responses.phonetic: day vee-NEEL, day vet-MAHN, day ah-FEESH ay day MUHBL ahn-SYAN
responses.french: Des vinyles, des vêtements, des affiches et des meubles anciens.
